normally don't like games that don't take long to defeat however this was one of the best ones i've ever played

I played it with my controllers and it was fine until I had to take the girls hand it litterally won’t let me and I got rid of my controllers for hand tracking and it still would let me so I can’t play on

reviews.review_title: I totally lived this life before
reviews.review_description: I totally love this game can't wait for a part 2 feel very connected to this game in a recarnation type of way only old souls know what I mean . Good job
The story line behind it all is 10/10 but the fighting could use some work

The game is incredibly simple and childish with poorly designed combat and virtually no story. The hand tracking is absolutely terrible, making the punching (which is the only real offense option you got for some reason) completely impractical and massively irritating. It absolutely is NOT playable in any seated or stationary position as the enemy seem to be designed to only appear directly behind the player forcing you to constantly rotate. Perhaps most daunting is the fact that you don't even need to be good at the game to play or even look at the screen, you can look down and keep pulling your hands apart and after hours, you will 100% the game, there's not even any penalty at all as if the game decides you are overwhelmed it automatically takes you to a safe space. There is also extremely minimal differences in any of the characters or the boss and the level are exactly the same. It takes less than 5 minutes to realize just how much of your time you've wasted by downloading this pile of game. It is nothing but good intentions and poor design in every aspect, not worth it at all.
Extremely interesting game but way too short.

Hi! I’m trying to play Fight Back VR on Meta Quest 3, but after launching the game, it only shows the intro menu (“Begin Journey”) and nothing responds — neither controllers nor hand gestures. I’ve already tried restarting the headset and reinstalling the game, but the problem persists. Is this a known issue on Quest 3? Is there a workaround or upcoming fix? Thanks!

developer_response.body: We are sorry to hear you're having issues! This is not a bug we're familiar with, it looks like a problem with the headset, not the game. Did you try updating your OS? Good luck!

reviews.review_title: THIS GAME IS A MUST FOR WOMEN/MINORITIES
reviews.review_description: this game not only will keep you entertained, but also safe. I would rate it higher if I could.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for your kind words!

reviews.review_description: When hand tracking gets better this is a 5 star. Really cool especially if you're into sacred geometry.
Like the stuff on it it’s awesome and like you doing it and doing it and doing it and doing it and doing it it’s amazing it’s amazing game play it now ￼￼
This the best meta quest game I’ve ever seen
Sure there’s a few glitches here and there but it’s a free game, so it’s worth it

I can get as far as standing in space with the title in front of me, figured out that I need to reach through the portals to get anywhere, then it all goes to black, nothing else happens. Have tried it countless times and have tried rebooting my Q3. Headset at V.69.0.0… Developers, please help.

So, 27 days later, I still can’t get into this and the developers don’t seem to know either. The initial graphics are so nice, I really want to see what’s next!

developer_response.body: Hello, thank your for your feedback. We'll check the application against the information provided. We hope to solve your problem and perhaps enable you to give a better rating later on.

reviews.review_description: The visual design is good, and the the gameplay idea is sound.  However, the gesture recognition just doesn't work well enough (at least for me).   As often as not, my gesture is not recognized, and there's no indication of what I need to do differently to have it recognized.  (One possibility is showing you a cleaned-up version of your gesture, and having sloppy drawings be less effective.)
Enjoyable to play, and learn.
Affe Affe 🐒

The environment is beautiful and well designed. 
There are quite a few hand tracking games but this one didn’t work well. It didn’t recognize most of the gestures and the combat mechanics was clunky. 
Another thing that bothered me was the use of a religious symbol as the darkness/shadow (enemy). Not in this time of our life time when too much race and religion based discrimination is rising.

developer_response.body: Hi neyume, we are at your disposal to help you on the hand tracking issues : the experience has to been done in a well-lit space and keeping the gestures in front of the headsets cameras. Please note that the game does not include any religious consideration at all.

reviews.review_description: Fighting actions can be improved
This game is great I love the story it really made me feel like docter strange. The Hans tracking was amazing Ioved it.

reviews.review_title: Good hand tracking and voice acting
reviews.review_description:
The game takes about 15 minutes to beat. I played it with the controllers and with hand tracking. 

The game was actually easier to control with hand tracking.

Note: Your may not track properly during the into, but don't worry. It will fix itself before gameplay begins.

Finished this today and I’ve got to say that this game was well made. I like the use of the narrating voice at beginning getting more and more negative, as it made me realize during one of the combat sections; 

 “This is our character being negative.”
And I also like the fact, despite VR being fairly limited for unarmed combat without FBT, the Self-Defense Moves were still implemented well. (I will admit, though, even after the Hand Tracking Updates to quest, that controllers were better because it tracked better, but Hand-Tracking  was fine overall.)


Only gripe that I have is that there doesn’t seem to be a mode where I can use all of the Moves with difficulty of my choice. Would definitely play the game again if they allowed this, but it’s whatever.

I suspect the 1-star reviews haven't actually finished the game (it's like 15 minutes long. Better get checked for ADD). Visuals are top shelf. Hand tracking works but is a little clunky. But the message at the end is worth it. I didn't expect to cry in VR today!

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for this!

reviews.review_description: Brilliant approach of self-defense experience in VR with a beautiful storytelling. Congrats!
amazing art and storytelling. Definitely worth 5 stars

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for your kind words!

reviews.review_description:
The message and substance of this experience is so thoughtful and meaningful. It is rare to see such an important message delivered in VR and I hope we see more of it.

Great use of hand tracking and spatial audio. I found the experience to be easy, intuitive, and inspirational.

UPDATE: I got the hand tracking to work, although functionality was shaky at best. In fact, it was so bad that I stopped playing mid-game. As I was being relentlessly pummeled by the enemy while hopelessly attempting to successfully form the hand gesture to quit the app, the guide kept cheering me on by exclaiming, "Good job! You're doing great!" That should tell you all you need to know.

I picked up this game(?) Because of the appealing graphics and supposed hand tracking action mechanics. However, I never made it through to any of the actual gameplay. That's how laughably cheesy the narrative is. The guide's voice and the writing are just...bad. Maybe I'll try to see past that and give it another shot.

More importantly, though: hand tracking didn't seem to work for me. After selecting hand tracking as the mode of controls, my hands were never detected and I couldn't select anything or proceed to the game at all. It never seemed to detect my hands, or the settings didn't engage. How exactly is the hand tracking supposed to work?

developer_response.body:
Hi QuackBirth,
Thanks for downloading Fight Back and taking time to write a review. 
We are sorry that you did not enjoy the experience, but we appreciate that you gave it a chance in the first place, so thank you.
The developers have done many tests to adjust the hand tracking. It seems to work for most users.Could you please tell us more about the issue you encountered a francetvlab.apps@gmail.com?
Hand tracking is supposed to work during all the experiences and and also in the menus if the feature is enabled in your Quest 2 settings.
We would be grateful if you could give it another shot. 

Cheers.
Fight Back Team.

reviews.review_description:
Messy controls even with the controllers, creepy ghost kid voice as a companion, too much exposition and about as fun to play as having one's teeth pulled out. 
Graphics aren't bad but there's a lot of work to do.

developer_response.body:
Hi Yannick,

Thanks for downloading Fight Back and taking time to write a review. 
As it is explained in the introduction titles, gestures should not be performed too quickly, with keeping your hands in sight of the heaset, was it ok for you? 
You can tell us more about the issue you encountered a francetvlab.apps@gmail.com 
We would be grateful if you can help us to make the experience better and could give it another try. 

Cheers.
Fight Back Team.

reviews.review_description: It feels like it’s targeted towards women and it’s very short so please dev add more I’m begging.

reviews.review_title: I LOVE THE GAME BUT I NEED HELP
reviews.review_description:
I loved playing this game or experience depending on how you look at it =D it's fun it's entertaining Something short but it's ok the hand tracking is good and everything works great a great experience that everyone should try this is why when I saw I had achievements I wanted to complete them all, that's why I said I need help as I have tried and they are broken it doesn't give me any achievements I know it's silly but I hope you can fix it =3

Update: still not working ;(

Thank you very much

developer_response.body: Thank you for reporting this issue! We are investigating it !
